search_queries_used: site:gov.ae chronic kidney disease follow-up guideline UAE
site:kdigo.org 2024 CKD guideline GFR albuminuria monitoring medication review referral
site:kdigo.org 2024 CKD Figure 48 referral albuminuria potassium
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Treatment algorithms and doses were prohibited.
Dialysis care is outside this follow-up documentation workflow.
population_applicability: Partial: KDIGO includes adult and paediatric qualifiers, CKD stage, cause, treatment, progression, and complication contexts not encoded in the broad workflow.
setting_applicability: Partial: exact for selected longitudinal review and referral documentation, not diagnosis, treatment, or fixed local follow-up pathways.
UAE_applicability: No exact current UAE CKD follow-up guideline was identified. KDIGO is international and local referral or monitoring implementation requires UAE clinician review.
recency_verification: The current official KDIGO 2024 guideline and its current-status page were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: KDIGO 2024 supersedes the 2012 CKD guideline; a focused Chapter 3 update is underway but was not yet published.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped CKD, renal-function, eGFR, prior-trend, and confirmation context without diagnosing CKD from one result.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports GFR and urine-albumin testing, repeat confirmation, prior GFR or albuminuria trends, imaging, history, and the minimum chronicity context without diagnosing CKD from one result.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped urinary, medicine, nephrotoxic, electrolyte, urinalysis, and clinical-context fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports careful medical, family, medicine, urinary, systemic, examination, urinalysis, ACR, electrolyte, and imaging context in clinician-led CKD evaluation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped GFR and ACR trend review and clinician-arranged reassessment after material change without fixed legacy intervals.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped medication-list, adherence, review, monitoring, interaction, and nephrotoxic-context fields without treatment changes.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports periodic medication review, accurate medication lists, adherence, continued indication, interaction, monitoring, reconciliation, and nephrotoxic-medicine context without treatment changes or doses.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped refractory-hypertension, potassium, albuminuria-with-decline, and clinician-decided referral fields only with Figure 48 qualifiers.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led referral documentation for qualified sustained GFR decline, significant albuminuria with haematuria, refractory hypertension, persistent potassium abnormalities, and other listed circumstances.

unresolved_source_gaps: Oedema, weight, fluid-balance, dietary, bone-mineral, general safety-netting, fixed three-to-twelve-month intervals, PRN wording, and the combined history draft remain unsupported by the mapped exact sections.
The workflow does not encode CKD stage, cause, age, albuminuria category, progression threshold, treatment context, or referral qualifiers.
Diagnosis, treatment, medicine changes or doses, referral urgency, and exact monitoring timing remain clinician responsibilities.
